label: BFSI Threat Intelligence Dashboard
description: Real-time threat intelligence dashboard for BFSI sector - IOC monitoring, threat actor tracking, and alert correlation
author: Anvesh Raju Vishwaraju

panels.title: 🔴 Active High-Risk IOCs (Last 24h)
panels.search: index=threat_intel sourcetype=ioc_feed risk_level=HIGH earliest=-24h | stats count by ioc_type, indicator, source, risk_level | sort -count

panels.title: 📊 IOC Types Distribution
panels.search: index=threat_intel sourcetype=ioc_feed earliest=-7d | stats count by ioc_type

panels.title: 🌐 Top Threat Source Countries
panels.search: index=threat_intel sourcetype=ioc_feed ioc_type=ip earliest=-7d | iplocation indicator | stats count by Country | sort -count | head 10

panels.title: 🎯 MITRE ATT&CK Technique Frequency
panels.search: index=threat_intel sourcetype=ioc_feed earliest=-30d | stats count by mitre_technique | sort -count | head 10

panels.title: ⚡ IOC Matches in Network Logs (Last 1h)
panels.search: index=network earliest=-1h [search index=threat_intel sourcetype=ioc_feed risk_level=HIGH | fields indicator | rename indicator as dest_ip] | table _time, src_ip, dest_ip, dest_port, bytes_out | sort -_time

panels.title: 📈 Threat Intel Feed Volume (7 Days)
panels.search: index=threat_intel sourcetype=ioc_feed earliest=-7d | timechart span=1d count by source

panels.title: 🏦 BFSI-Targeted Campaigns Active
panels.search: index=threat_intel sourcetype=ioc_feed industries=BFSI earliest=-7d | stats dc(campaign_id) as active_campaigns

panels.title: 🚨 Firewall Hits on Known Bad IPs
panels.search: index=network sourcetype=firewall_logs [search index=threat_intel sourcetype=ioc_feed ioc_type=ip risk_level=HIGH | fields indicator | rename indicator as dest_ip] earliest=-24h | stats count by src_ip, dest_ip, action | sort -count

setup: 2. Run enrichment-script.py to populate IOC data
3. Import this dashboard: Splunk → Dashboards → Import
4. Adjust index names to match your environment
5. Set dashboard refresh to every 5 minutes
instructions.note: Replace index names (threat_intel, network) with your actual Splunk index names
